headline: How Bernard Arnault works to keep his media outlets in line
description: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (4/6). Since the 1990s, the CEO of LVMH has built a small media galaxy in print and radio, which he uses to convey his ideas and tastes, but also to promote his brands.

headline: Trump administration seeks additional funding for its war against Iran

US trial of Venezuela's Maduro set for June 2027
description: Venezuela's ousted president and his wife will go on trial next year to face charges that include 'narco-terrorism' conspiracy and cocaine importation conspiracy. Maduro also faces a civil case in the US that alleges he commanded an elite security force to execute five young men between 2017 and 2020 as part of a wider pattern of state violence.

headline: Kremlin propagandist Xenia Fedorova escapes EU sanctions targeting Russian figures
description: The French Foreign Ministry and Interior Ministry are quietly exploring alternative sanctions against the pro-Russian CNews personality, former head of Russia Today France, a channel funded by the Kremlin.

headline: European Commission withdraws €2 million grant from Venice Biennale over Russian presence
description: The major contemporary art event had allowed Russia to return to the festival and present a pavilion there in May, before closing it to the public, sparking controversy and leading the festival's jury to resign.

headline: Friterie des Nations, a local institution at the Calais Beach
description: Gourmet kiosks (1/6). Across France, small kiosks serving local specialties are a favorite stop for holidaymakers. In Calais, northern France, Pascal Carré and his daughter Loranne serve fries that are crisp on the outside and soft on the inside.

How social media bans for young users, now in effect in France, have spread worldwide
description: France has decided to ban those under 15 from major platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. The country is following a global trend that has gained momentum in recent years.

headline: New monkey species identified in Lomami National Park, Democratic Republic of Congo
description: First photographed in 2008, this small colobus monkey with pink-orange lips has now been officially described by an international team of researchers – and may be immediately classified as 'endangered.'

headline: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
description: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.
